mnemonics.title: Page Table Entry Bits
mnemonics.content: DIP V M R W: DIRTY BIT, INVALID BIT, PROTECTION BIT, VALID BIT, MODIFIED BIT, REFERENCE BIT (USAGE BITS)

mnemonics.title: Page Replacement Algorithms
mnemonics.content: FIFO: FIRST IN FIRST OUT - OLDEST PAGE OUT; LRU: LEAST RECENTLY USED - OLDEST IN TIME OUT; OPTIMAL: LOOKS FORWARD - FURTHEST FUTURE USE OUT

mnemonics.title: Memory Management Terms
mnemonics.content: MEMORY MANAGEMENT KEEPS TRACK: ALLOCATION (GIVE MEMORY), DEALLOCATION (TAKE BACK), PROTECTION (KEEP SAFE), SHARING (LET OTHERS USE)

acronyms.usage: Hardware cache for fast virtual-to-physical address translation
Entry in page table containing frame number and control bits
Page replacement algorithm replacing page used longest ago
Page replacement algorithm replacing oldest page
Average time to access memory with paging and TLB
Allows devices to access memory without CPU intervention

visualTips.visualization: Draw a box split into PAGE NUMBER (left) and OFFSET (right). ARROW from page number through PAGE TABLE to FRAME NUMBER. COMBINE frame number with offset to get physical address.
DRAW A FLOWCHART: PROCESS ACCESS → CHECK TLB → MISS → CHECK PAGE TABLE → INVALID? → PAGE FAULT → OS LOADS FROM DISK → UPDATE PAGE TABLE → RESUME PROCESS.

visualTips.visualize: CIRCLE DIAGRAM: HIGH DEGREE OF MULTIPROGRAMMING → TOO MANY PROCESSES → NOT ENOUGH FRAMES → HIGH PAGE FAULTS → DISK I/O → CPU IDLE → MORE SWAPPING → MORE THRASHING.
TWO SIDE-BY-SIDE BOXES: PAGING = UNIFORM ROWS (FIXED SIZE PAGES); SEGMENTATION = IRREGULAR ROWS (VARIABLE SIZES). SEGMENTS HAVE NAMES, PAGES HAVE NUMBERS.

analogies.analogy: THINK OF A BOOK WHERE EACH CHAPTER IS DIVIDED INTO PAGES. YOU DON'T NEED TO READ THE WHOLE BOOK AT ONCE - YOU BRING SPECIFIC PAGES (PAGES) TO YOUR DESK (FRAMES) AS NEEDED.
LIKE A LIBRARY'S HOLDING SYSTEM. YOU CAN REQUEST ANY BOOK (PROGRAM) EVEN IF IT'S NOT IN THE LIBRARY (RAM). THEY'LL GET IT FOR YOU FROM STORAGE (DISK) WHEN YOU NEED IT.
IMAGINE A STUDENT WITH TOO MANY TEXTBOOKS TO CARRY. THEY SPEND MORE TIME SWAPPING BOOKS IN AND OUT OF THEIR BAG THAN ACTUALLY READING THEM.
LIKE A MAP OR INDEX IN A大型 BOOK. IT TELLS YOU WHICH PAGE NUMBER (VIRTUAL) CORRESPONDS TO WHICH ACTUAL BOOK PAGE (PHYSICAL FRAME).
